notes: Antigravity workflow fan-out, UNBLOCKED by subagent linking. The parent uses its multi-agent feature ([Project Orchestrator] / invoke_subagent with a Subagents array holding several entries) to fan out ≈3 parallel children in one call. Each child runs as its OWN top-level brain/<child>/ conversation (transcript_new per child), all linked to the SAME parent via AntigravityParentConvID → parent_linked per child. SubagentCount(parent)=N peaks while children run, decrements as each ends, parent working→ready only after the last child, then children are swept (SubagentCount returns to 0). UNLIKE claudecode there is NO per-run subdir and NO journal/bookkeeping file — children are flat top-level conversations — so the 'journal/unknown never surfaces as a phantom session' guard is VACUOUSLY satisfied for antigravity. The fan-out children have no own agy process (pid=0, transcript-first) — assert ParentSessionID linkage + the N-count, not child PIDs. Model harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> ('Gemini 3.1 Pro'). Tokens/cost NOT in the antigravity JSONL.

text: Parent conv-id session appears in ready when transcript.jsonl is written (single-birth, no proc-<PID> presession)
The workflow opt-in prompt drives the parent ready → working as the fan-out starts
Each fan-out child's own conversation transcript materializes at brain/<child>/.system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl — fswatcher discovers a new SessionState per child

AntigravityParentConvID binds each child.session_id → the SAME parent.session_id (children are subagents of the parent, NOT top-level sessions). SubagentCount(parent) peaks at ~3 while children run; no 'journal'/'unknown' phantom session arises (vacuous for antigravity — no per-run subdir or bookkeeping file).

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while the fan-out children run — reevaluateParent holds it on hasActiveChildren
Parent transitions working → ready only after the last child ends; children have transitioned working → ready as they finished (SubagentCount decremented to 0)
The fan-out children are swept like ordinary child sessions, returning SubagentCount(parent) to 0 — no stale subagent badge
